name: Proposal
about: Make the case for a change before writing the code

---

Read the [MANIFESTO](../../MANIFESTO.md) before you open this. The default answer to "should Cadence do one more thing" is no, and it has to be. That is not a discouragement, it is the ground rule, and a good proposal argues against it on purpose.

## What you want Cadence to do

## Why the default no is wrong here

What does this earn that the current surface can't, and what would fail if Cadence kept refusing it?

## What it costs

New command, config key, agent, or track? What can now fail that couldn't before?

## Why this belongs in `main` and not a fork

The license is MIT. If the honest answer is "it fits my workflow," a fork is the right home. Make the case that it fits everyone's.
